title: Compute Vector Norm
description: Algorithm to compute Euclidean norm (length) of a vector
code:
FUNCTION norm(vector v)
 sum_squares ← 0
 FOR each component vi IN v
 sum_squares ← sum_squares + vi * vi
 RETURN SQRT(sum_squares)

# Example: v = [3, 4]
# norm = √(9 + 16) = √25 = 5
explanation: The Euclidean norm computes the magnitude of a vector by taking square root of sum of squares of all components.

title: Compute Dot Product
description: Algorithm to compute inner product (dot product) of two vectors
code:
FUNCTION dotProduct(vector u, vector v)
 result ← 0
 FOR i FROM 0 TO length(u) - 1
 result ← result + u[i] * v[i]
 RETURN result

# Example: u = [1,2,3], v = [2,-1,0]
# dot = 1*2 + 2*(-1) + 3*0 = 0 (orthogonal!)
explanation: Dot product sums the products of corresponding components. Result = 0 indicates orthogonal vectors.

title: Gram-Schmidt Orthogonalization
description: Convert linearly independent vectors to orthogonal set
code:
FUNCTION gramSchmidt(vectors U)
 vectors V ← empty list
 
 FOR i FROM 0 TO U.length - 1
 temp ← U[i]
 
 FOR j FROM 0 TO V.length - 1
 # Subtract projections onto previous orthogonal vectors
 proj ← dotProduct(U[i], V[j]) / dotProduct(V[j], V[j])
 temp ← temp - proj * V[j]
 
 V.append(temp)
 
 RETURN V

# Returns orthogonal set spanning same subspace
explanation: Each new vector is made orthogonal to all previous vectors by subtracting their projections. Result vectors are mutually orthogonal.

title: Compute Vector Projection
description: Algorithm to find projection of vector u onto vector v
code:
FUNCTION projection(vector u, vector v)
 dot_uv ← dotProduct(u, v)
 dot_vv ← dotProduct(v, v)
 
 IF dot_vv = 0 THEN
 RETURN error "Cannot project onto zero vector"
 
 scalar ← dot_uv / dot_vv
 
 # Scale v by the scalar
 result ← scalar * v
 RETURN result

# proj_v(u) = (u·v / v·v) v
explanation: Projection finds the component of u in the direction of v. The scalar tells us how much of u goes in v's direction.

title: Normalize Vector to Unit Vector
description: Convert any non-zero vector to unit vector (length 1)
code:
FUNCTION normalize(vector v)
 v_norm ← norm(v)
 
 IF v_norm = 0 THEN
 RETURN error "Cannot normalize zero vector"
 
 unit ← []
 FOR each vi IN v
 unit.append(vi / v_norm)
 
 RETURN unit

# Now ||unit|| = 1
explanation: Dividing a vector by its norm gives a unit vector pointing in the same direction with length 1.
